what: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition
title: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition
text: Exploration analysis compositional data framework Aitchison (1986). package provides tools chemical fingerprinting source tracking ancient materials. Initial development progress.

title: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition
text: can install released version nexus CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("nexus") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/nexus")

title: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition
text: nexus provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix. CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency data (composition data), LogRatio represents log-ratio matrix. assumes keep data tidy: variable must saved column observation (sample) must saved row. new classes simple use inherit base matrix: CompositionMatrix LogRatio classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area. coercing data.frame CompositionMatrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names. behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE).
code: ## Load the package library(nexus) ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite) X <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 75, TRUE), nrow = 15, ncol = 5) X <- as.data.frame(X) X$samples <- sample(c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"), 15, TRUE) X$groups <- sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 15, TRUE)  ## Coerce to a count matrix Y <- as_composition(X)  ## Get groups get_samples(Y) #>  [1] "d" "c" "c" "d" "e" "c" "b" "a" "c" "a" "b" "e" "a" "d" "d"  ## Get groups get_groups(Y) #>  [1] "A" "C" "C" "B" "C" "A" "B" "A" "A" "A" "C" "A" "B" "B" "C"

title: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition
text: Please note nexus project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Compositional Matrix — CompositionMatrix-class
text: totals numeric vector store absolute row sums (closure compositions). samples character vector store sample identifiers (allows duplicates case replicated measurements). groups character vector store group names ().

title: Log-Ratio Matrix — LogRatio-class
text: S4 classes represent log-ratio data transformations.

title: Log-Ratio Matrix — LogRatio-class
text: parts character vector store part names. ratio character vector store ratio names. order integer vector store original ordering columns. base numeric matrix store basis transformation. weights numeric vector store weights assigned respective log-ratios.

title: Numeric Matrix — NumericMatrix-class
text: S4 classes represent \(m \times p\) numeric matrix.

title: Outliers — OutlierIndex-class
text: S4 class store result outlier detection.

title: Outliers — OutlierIndex-class
text: samples character vector store sample identifiers (allows duplicates case replicated measurements). groups character vector store group names (). distances numeric vector giving squared Mahalanobis distance. limit numeric value giving cut-value used outlier detection. robust logical scalar: robust estimators used?

title: Coerce — coerce
code: as_count(from, ...)  as_composition(from, ...)  # S4 method for matrix as_composition(from)  # S4 method for data.frame as_composition(from, samples = NULL, groups = NULL)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix as_count(from)

title: Coerce — coerce
text: object coerced. ... Currently used. samples integer giving index column used sample identification: allows identify replicated measurements. NULL (default), row names used sample IDs. groups integer giving index column used group samples. NULL (default), grouping stored.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: following methods available: CompositionMatrix class special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area. coercing data.frame CompositionMatrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names (case insensitive, plural insensitive). behavior can disabled setting options(nexus.autodetect = FALSE) overrided explicitly specifying columns used as_composition().

title: Coerce — coerce
code: ## Create a count matrix A1 <- matrix(data = as.numeric(sample(1:100, 100, TRUE)), nrow = 20)  ## Coerce to compositions B <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to relative frequencies ## (use get_totals() to retrieve these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B)  ## Coerce to an S3 data.frame X <- data.frame(B) head(X) #>            col1       col2        col3       col4       col5 samples #> row1 0.20930233 0.28903654 0.312292359 0.13289037 0.05647841    row1 #> row2 0.27859238 0.20821114 0.266862170 0.02932551 0.21700880    row2 #> row3 0.16722408 0.19397993 0.177257525 0.24080268 0.22073579    row3 #> row4 0.24555160 0.28825623 0.003558719 0.21352313 0.24911032    row4 #> row5 0.20606061 0.30000000 0.057575758 0.26060606 0.17575758    row5 #> row6 0.06741573 0.08988764 0.284644195 0.32584270 0.23220974    row6

title: Variance and Covariance — covariance
text: var() computes log-ratio variance matrix. cov() computes log-ratio covariance matrix.

title: Distances — dist
text: x CompositionMatrix object. method character string specifying distance measure used. See stats::dist() available distances. diag logical scalar indicating whether diagonal distance matrix printed. upper logical scalar indicating whether upper triangle distance matrix printed. p integer giving power Minkowski distance.

title: Distances — dist
text: Aitchison, J. (1986). Statistical Analysis Compositional Data. London: Chapman Hall, p. 64-91. doi:10.1007/978-94-009-4109-0 . Greenacre, M. J. (2019). Compositional Data Analysis Practice. Boca Raton: CRC Press.

title: Graph of Log-ratios — graph
code: graph(object, ...)  # S4 method for LR graph(object)  # S4 method for ALR graph(object)  # S4 method for ILR graph(object)

title: Graph of Log-ratios — graph
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Pairwise log-ratio lr <- transform_lr(coda) lr_graph <- graph(lr) plot(lr_graph)   ## Additive log-ratio alr <- transform_alr(coda) alr_graph <- graph(alr) plot(alr_graph)   ## Isometric log-ratio ilr <- transform_ilr(coda) ilr_graph <- graph(ilr) plot(ilr_graph)   plr <- transform_plr(coda) plr_graph <- graph(plr) plot(plr_graph)

what: Geological Model Age from Lead Isotope Analysis — lia_age
title: Geological Model Age from Lead Isotope Analysis — lia_age
text: Compute geological model age (T) U/Pb (mu) Th/U (kappa) ratios lead isotopic measurements.

title: Geological Model Age from Lead Isotope Analysis — lia_age
code: lia_age(x, y, z, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric,numeric lia_age(   x,   y,   z,   t0 = 3.8,   x_star = 18.75,   y_star = 15.63,   z_star = 38.86,   mu = 9.66,   kappa = 3.9,   th232 = 0.049475,   u238 = 0.155125,   u235 = 0.98485,   u238_235 = 137.79,   tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) )  # S4 method for list,missing,missing lia_age(   x,   t0 = 3.8,   x_star = 18.75,   y_star = 15.63,   z_star = 38.86,   mu = 9.66,   kappa = 3.9,   th232 = 0.049475,   u238 = 0.155125,   u235 = 0.98485,   u238_235 = 137.79,   tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) )

title: Geological Model Age from Lead Isotope Analysis — lia_age
text: x numeric vector 206Pb/204Pb ratios. y z missing, must list (data.frame) numeric components (columns) x, y z. y numeric vector 207Pb/204Pb ratios.  missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way. z numeric vector 208Pb/204Pb ratios.  missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way. ... Currently used. t0 numeric value giving time second stage reference model. x_star numeric value giving 206Pb/204Pb ratio \(t = 0\). y_star numeric value giving 207Pb/204Pb ratio \(t = 0\). z_star numeric value giving 208Pb/204Pb ratio \(t = 0\). mu numeric value giving 238U/204Pb ratio reference model. kappa numeric value giving 232Th/238U ratio reference model. th232 numeric value giving decay constants 232Th. u238 numeric value giving decay constants 238U. u235 numeric value giving decay constants 235U. u238_235 numeric value giving 238U/235U ratio. tolerance numeric value specifying tolerance (stopping criteria Newton–Raphson method).

title: Geological Model Age from Lead Isotope Analysis — lia_age
text: four columns data.frame: age Geological model age (Ma). mu 238U/204Pb ratio. kappa 232Th/238U ratio. f

what: nexus: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition — nexus-package
title: nexus: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition — nexus-package
text: Exploration analysis compositional data framework Aitchison (1986) <isbn: 9780412280603>. package provides tools chemical fingerprinting source tracking ancient materials.

title: nexus: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition — nexus-package
text: nexus uses following options() configure behavior: nexus.autodetect: logical scalar. Try automatically assign values corresponding slot CompositionMatrix object coercing data.frame? Defaults TRUE. nexus.verbose: logical scalar. R report extra information progress? Defaults TRUE.

title: nexus: Sourcing Archaeological Materials by Chemical Composition — nexus-package

title: Outlier Detection — outliers
code: outliers(object, ...)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix outliers(   object,   center = NULL,   cov = NULL,   robust = TRUE,   alpha = 0.5,   level = 0.975 )

title: Outlier Detection — outliers
text: object CompositionMatrix. ... Currently used. center numeric vector giving mean vector distribution. missing, estimated x. cov numeric matrix giving covariance distribution. missing, estimated x. robust logical scalar: robust location scatter estimation used (see robustbase::covMcd())? alpha length-one numeric vector controlling size subsets determinant minimized (see robustbase::covMcd()). used robust TRUE. level length-one numeric vector giving significance level. level used cut-value outlier detection: observations larger (squared) Mahalanobis distance considered potential outliers.

title: Outlier Detection — outliers
text: outlier can defined large Mahalanobis distance observations. way, certain proportion observations can identified, e.g. top 2% values (.e. values 0.98th percentile Chi-2 distribution). one hand, Mahalanobis distance likely strongly affected presence outliers. Rousseeuw van Zomeren (1990) thus recommend using robust methods (excessively affected presence outliers). hand, choice threshold classifying observation outlier discussed. apparent reason particular threshold applicable data sets (Filzmoser, Garrett, Reimann 2005).

title: Outlier Detection — outliers
text: Filzmoser, P., Garrett, R. G. & Reimann, C. (2005). Multivariate outlier detection exploration geochemistry. Computers & Geosciences, 31(5), 579-587. doi:10.1016/j.cageo.2004.11.013 . Filzmoser, P. & Hron, K. (2008). Outlier Detection Compositional Data Using Robust Methods. Mathematical Geosciences, 40(3), 233-248. doi:10.1007/s11004-007-9141-5 . Filzmoser, P., Hron, K. & Reimann, C. (2012). Interpretation multivariate outliers compositional data. Computers & Geosciences, 39, 77-85. doi:10.1016/j.cageo.2011.06.014 . Rousseeuw, P. J. & van Zomeren, B. C. (1990). Unmasking Multivariate Outliers Leverage Points. Journal American Statistical Association, 85(411): 633-639. doi:10.1080/01621459.1990.10474920 . Santos, F. (2020). Modern methods old data: overview robust methods outliers detection applications osteology. Journal Archaeological Science: Reports, 32, 102423. doi:10.1016/j.jasrep.2020.102423 .

title: Outlier Detection — outliers
code: ## Coerce to chemical data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Detect outliers out <- outliers(coda)  ## Plot plot(out, qq = TRUE)  plot(out, qq = FALSE)

title: Principal Components Analysis — pca
text: Computes principal components analysis based singular value decomposition.

title: Principal Components Analysis — pca
code: # S4 method for CompositionMatrix pca(   object,   center = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   rank = NULL,   sup_row = NULL,   sup_col = NULL,   weight_row = NULL,   weight_col = NULL )  # S4 method for LogRatio pca(   object,   center = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   rank = NULL,   sup_row = NULL,   sup_col = NULL,   weight_row = NULL,   weight_col = NULL )

title: Principal Components Analysis — pca
text: object LogRatio object. center logical scalar: variables shifted zero centered? scale logical scalar: variables scaled unit variance? rank integer value specifying maximal number components kept results. NULL (default), \(p - 1\) components returned. sup_row numeric logical vector specifying indices supplementary rows (individuals). sup_col numeric logical vector specifying indices supplementary columns (variables). weight_row numeric vector specifying active row (individual) weights. NULL (default), uniform weights used. Row weights internally normalized sum 1 weight_col numeric vector specifying active column (variable) weights. NULL (default), uniform weights (1) used.

title: Principal Components Analysis — pca
code: ## Coerce to compositional data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Centered log-ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda)  ## PCA X <- pca(clr)  ## Plot plot_individuals(X)

title: Plot Compositional Data — plot_coda
code: # S4 method for CompositionMatrix autoplot(object, ..., order = NULL, decreasing = FALSE, facet = TRUE)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix,missing plot(x, order = NULL, decreasing = FALSE, facet = TRUE, ...)

title: Plot Compositional Data — plot_coda
text: ... Currently used. order integer vector giving index column used ordering data. decreasing logical scalar: sort order increasing decreasing? facet logical scalar: matrix panels defined groups drawn? used x groups defined. x, object CompositionMatrix object.

title: Plot Compositional Data — plot_coda
text: autoplot() returns ggplot object. plot() called side-effects: results graphic displayed (invisibly returns x).

title: Plot Compositional Data — plot_coda
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Bar plot plot(coda, order = 2)   ## Use ceramic types for grouping data("kommos", package = "folio") kommos <- remove_NA(kommos, margin = 1) # Remove cases with missing values coda <- as_composition(kommos, groups = 1) #> 1 qualitative variable was removed: date.  plot(coda, order = 1)   ## Log ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda) plot(clr, facet = FALSE)

title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
code: # S4 method for CompositionMatrix [(x, i, j, ..., drop = TRUE)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix [(x, i, j, ...) <- value  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix [[(x, i, j, ...) <- value

title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
text: x object extract element(s) replace element(s) (typically *Matrix object). , j Indices specifying elements extract replace. Indices numeric, integer character vectors empty (missing) NULL. Numeric values coerced integer .integer() (hence truncated towards zero). Character vectors matched name elements. empty index (comma separated blank) indicates entries dimension selected. ... Currently used. drop logical scalar: result coerced lowest possible dimension? works extracting elements, replacement. value possible value element(s) x.

Additive Log-Ratios (ALR) — transform_alr
code: transform_alr(object, ...)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix transform_alr(object, j = ncol(object))

title: Additive Log-Ratios (ALR) — transform_alr
text: object CompositionMatrix object. ... Currently used. j integer giving index rationing part (denominator).

title: Additive Log-Ratios (ALR) — transform_alr
text: Aitchison, J. (1986). Statistical Analysis Compositional Data. London: Chapman Hall. doi:10.1007/978-94-009-4109-0 . Greenacre, M. J. (2019). Compositional Data Analysis Practice. Boca Raton: CRC Press.

title: Additive Log-Ratios (ALR) — transform_alr
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Pairwise log-ratio lr <- transform_lr(coda)  ## Centered log-ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda)  ## Additive log-ratio alr <- transform_alr(coda)  ## Isometric log-ratio ilr <- transform_ilr(coda) plr <- transform_plr(coda)  ## Inverse transformation inv_clr <- transform_inverse(clr) all.equal(coda, inv_clr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_alr <- transform_inverse(alr) all.equal(coda, inv_alr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_ilr <- transform_inverse(ilr) all.equal(coda, inv_ilr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_plr <- transform_inverse(plr) all.equal(coda, inv_plr) #> [1] TRUE

title: Centered Log-Ratios (CLR) — transform_clr
code: transform_clr(object, ...)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix transform_clr(object, weights = FALSE)

title: Centered Log-Ratios (CLR) — transform_clr
text: object CompositionMatrix object. ... Currently used. weights logical scalar: sould varying weight used. FALSE (default), equally-weighted parts used. Alternatively, positive numeric vector weights can specified.

title: Centered Log-Ratios (CLR) — transform_clr
text: Aitchison, J. (1986). Statistical Analysis Compositional Data. London: Chapman Hall. doi:10.1007/978-94-009-4109-0 . Greenacre, M. J. (2019). Compositional Data Analysis Practice. Boca Raton: CRC Press.

title: Centered Log-Ratios (CLR) — transform_clr
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Pairwise log-ratio lr <- transform_lr(coda)  ## Centered log-ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda)  ## Additive log-ratio alr <- transform_alr(coda)  ## Isometric log-ratio ilr <- transform_ilr(coda) plr <- transform_plr(coda)  ## Inverse transformation inv_clr <- transform_inverse(clr) all.equal(coda, inv_clr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_alr <- transform_inverse(alr) all.equal(coda, inv_alr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_ilr <- transform_inverse(ilr) all.equal(coda, inv_ilr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_plr <- transform_inverse(plr) all.equal(coda, inv_plr) #> [1] TRUE

title: Isometric Log-Ratios (ILR) — transform_ilr
code: transform_ilr(object, ...)  # S4 method for CompositionMatrix transform_ilr(object)

title: Isometric Log-Ratios (ILR) — transform_ilr
text: ILR transformation provides coordinates composition respect given orthonormal basis. transform_ilr() uses orthonormal basis (Helmert matrix) originally defined Egozcue et al. (2003).

title: Isometric Log-Ratios (ILR) — transform_ilr
text: Egozcue, J. J., Pawlowsky-Glahn, V., Mateu-Figueras, G. & Barceló-Vidal, C. (2003). Isometric Logratio Transformations Compositional Data Analysis. Mathematical Geology, 35(3), 279-300. doi:10.1023/:1023818214614 . Greenacre, M. J. (2019). Compositional Data Analysis Practice. Boca Raton: CRC Press.

title: Isometric Log-Ratios (ILR) — transform_ilr
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Pairwise log-ratio lr <- transform_lr(coda)  ## Centered log-ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda)  ## Additive log-ratio alr <- transform_alr(coda)  ## Isometric log-ratio ilr <- transform_ilr(coda) plr <- transform_plr(coda)  ## Inverse transformation inv_clr <- transform_inverse(clr) all.equal(coda, inv_clr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_alr <- transform_inverse(alr) all.equal(coda, inv_alr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_ilr <- transform_inverse(ilr) all.equal(coda, inv_ilr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_plr <- transform_inverse(plr) all.equal(coda, inv_plr) #> [1] TRUE

title: Inverse Log-Ratio Transformation — transform_inverse
code: transform_inverse(object, ...)  # S4 method for CLR transform_inverse(object)  # S4 method for ALR transform_inverse(object)  # S4 method for ILR transform_inverse(object)

(2003). Isometric Logratio Transformations Compositional Data Analysis. Mathematical Geology, 35(3), 279-300. doi:10.1023/:1023818214614 . Fišerová, E. & Hron, K. (2011). Interpretation Orthonormal Coordinates Compositional Data. Mathematical Geosciences, 43(4), 455‑468. doi:10.1007/s11004-011-9333-x . Greenacre, M. J. (2019). Compositional Data Analysis Practice. Boca Raton: CRC Press.

title: Inverse Log-Ratio Transformation — transform_inverse
code: ## Coerce to compositonal data data("hongite") coda <- as_composition(hongite)  ## Pairwise log-ratio lr <- transform_lr(coda)  ## Centered log-ratio clr <- transform_clr(coda)  ## Additive log-ratio alr <- transform_alr(coda)  ## Isometric log-ratio ilr <- transform_ilr(coda) plr <- transform_plr(coda)  ## Inverse transformation inv_clr <- transform_inverse(clr) all.equal(coda, inv_clr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_alr <- transform_inverse(alr) all.equal(coda, inv_alr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_ilr <- transform_inverse(ilr) all.equal(coda, inv_ilr) #> [1] TRUE  inv_plr <- transform_inverse(plr) all.equal(coda, inv_plr) #> [1] TRUE
